title: Upstart stock jumps after Q4 earnings, 2026 guidance impress
text: Upstart beat analyst estimates on both EPS and revenue for Q4 2025 and guided 2026 revenue above consensus, but projected an adjusted EBITDA margin lower than consensus. Management announced it will stop providing quarterly financial guidance, opting instead to issue annual targets supplemented by monthly transaction volume updates. For the 3-year period spanning 2025 to 2028, management is targeting to maintain a 35% compound annual growth rate with a terminal adjusted EBITDA margin of around 25%. Based on 22 Wall Street analysts, UPST has a bullish consensus with a median price target of $45.00 (ranging from $20.00 to $80.00), with an overall analyst rating of Buy (7.7/10). Upstart Holdings is forecast to grow earnings and revenue by 101.4% and 20% per annum respectively, with EPS expected to grow by 101% per annum, and return on equity forecasted to be 25.2% in 3 years. The guidance shift has created some uncertainty, as the aggressive pivot into secured loans (auto and home) is dragging down contribution margins, raising concerns about near-term profitability despite expanding the Total Addressable Market.
